files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/pipeline.py
@@ -23,10 +23,14 @@
 
 @router.post("/run", status_code=202)
 async def start_run(
     body: PipelineRunRequest,
     background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
 ) -> dict:
     """
     Start a new pipeline run in the background.
     """
+    # Input Validation Guard
+    if not body.diff_text or len(body.diff_text.strip()) < 10:
+        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="diff_text must be at least 10 characters long.")
+        
     orchestrator = PipelineOrchestrator()
     # A temporary orchestration is needed to get the run_id before starting

scenarios.title: Successfully trigger a new pipeline run via API
scenarios.description: Verify that a POST request to /api/pipeline/run returns a 202 status and a valid run_id.
scenarios.expected_result: The pipeline starts in the background and returns a tracking ID immediately.

scenarios.title: Monitor pipeline progress via SSE stream
scenarios.description: Ensure the client can connect to the SSE endpoint using the run_id from a fresh run.

steps.assertion: Receive at least one event from the SSE bus

scenarios.expected_result: Connection remains open and streams events related to the specific run_id.

scenarios.title: Verify completed pipeline appears in reports list
scenarios.description: Check the reports dashboard to ensure the metadata for the completed run is correctly stored and retrieved.
preconditions: A pipeline run has completed successfully

steps.assertion: matches the ID of the completed run

scenarios.expected_result: The summary list includes the label, status, and execution counts for the run.

scenarios.title: Handle missing request body in pipeline run
scenarios.description: Verify that the API returns a validation error when no payload is provided to the /run route.
scenarios.expected_result: FastAPI returns an Unprocessable Entity error due to missing required schema fields.

scenarios.title: Handle invalid JSON structure in run request
scenarios.description: Submit malformed JSON to ensure the server handles parsing errors gracefully.
scenarios.expected_result: The server rejects the malformed JSON with an appropriate error message.

scenarios.title: Concurrent pipeline run isolation
scenarios.description: Trigger multiple runs rapidly and verify unique event buses are created.
scenarios.expected_result: System handles multiple background tasks simultaneously without ID collisions.

scenarios.title: Resilience against corrupted report files
scenarios.description: Verify that list_reports skips invalid JSON files in the reports directory without crashing.
preconditions: A non-JSON or corrupted .json file exists in the REPORTS_DIR
scenarios.expected_result: The API returns valid reports and logs a warning for the corrupted file as per the exception handler.

scenarios.title: Empty reports state handling
scenarios.description: Verify the behavior when no reports have been generated yet.
preconditions: REPORTS_DIR is empty or does not exist
scenarios.expected_result: The API handles the absence of a directory/files gracefully.

scenarios.title: Prevent XSS via pipeline labels in report summary
scenarios.description: Test if a malicious script injected in the run label is sanitized when listed in reports.

steps.assertion: Response contains the literal string and is not executed

scenarios.expected_result: Malicious input is treated as plain text data.

scenarios.title: Validate run details for non-existent run_id
scenarios.description: Attempt to fetch a specific report that does not exist.
scenarios.expected_result: The API returns a Not Found error.

import os
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

# BASE_URL configuration
BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")

# SCENARIO_ID: 24023000-338a-4645-9f73-ba3d7ddb35d2
def test_successfully_trigger_new_pipeline_run_via_api(page: Page):
    # TODO: resolve PipelineRunRequest
    pytest.skip("payload model PipelineRunRequest not in context")

# SCENARIO_ID: d0d9dd23-99d7-458a-8b03-ab30579b2d82
def test_monitor_pipeline_progress_via_sse_stream(page: Page):
    # TODO: resolve PipelineRunRequest
    pytest.skip("payload model PipelineRunRequest not in context")

# SCENARIO_ID: fdedf6db-d683-41f9-8d03-df718237491d
def test_verify_completed_pipeline_appears_in_reports_list(page: Page):
    reports_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports"
    response = page.goto(reports_url)
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    
    # Verify that the JSON response contains expected keys using text locator
    # Since the response is raw JSON, Playwright renders it in the document body
    expect(page.get_by_text("run_id")).to_be_visible()
    expect(page.get_by_text("status")).to_be_visible()

# SCENARIO_ID: b40fec14-9435-42ab-9b61-315bff9c19ea
def test_handle_missing_request_body_in_pipeline_run(page: Page):
    run_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run"
    # Trigger POST with empty body
    response = page.request.post(run_url, data="")
    expect(response).not_to_be_ok()
    assert response.status == 422

# SCENARIO_ID: 0588c5fd-7e8b-484b-b41e-27f788d34819
def test_handle_invalid_json_structure_in_run_request(page: Page):
    run_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/pipeline/run"
    # Trigger POST with malformed JSON string
    response = page.request.post(
        run_url, 
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}, 
        data='{"malformed": json}'
    )
    expect(response).not_to_be_ok()
    assert response.status in [400, 422]

# SCENARIO_ID: 80953c59-8bb5-45bc-b7bf-d9931b8cccd7
def test_concurrent_pipeline_run_isolation(page: Page):
    # TODO: resolve PipelineRunRequest
    pytest.skip("payload model PipelineRunRequest not in context")

# SCENARIO_ID: 350fc590-9ba5-4ef1-b0c8-e4d7da4b5cef
def test_resilience_against_corrupted_report_files(page: Page):
    reports_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports"
    response = page.goto(reports_url)
    # The API should handle internal parsing errors gracefully and return 200 for remaining valid files
    expect(response).to_be_ok()

# SCENARIO_ID: 0d5ce6d6-d645-4c41-b8d1-87272b68d1ef
def test_empty_reports_state_handling(page: Page):
    reports_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports"
    response = page.goto(reports_url)
    expect(response).to_be_ok()
    
    # Verify that the response body contains an empty list representation
    expect(page.get_by_text("[]")).to_be_visible()

# SCENARIO_ID: ef7a97ac-cbfd-48e8-85a6-5a1d94537e83
def test_prevent_xss_via_pipeline_labels_in_report_summary(page: Page):
    # TODO: resolve PipelineRunRequest
    pytest.skip("payload model PipelineRunRequest not in context")

# SCENARIO_ID: 640bde53-04c0-4e86-aea0-d326829abe52
def test_validate_run_details_for_non_existent_run_id(page: Page):
    invalid_run_id = "non-existent-uuid"
    report_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/reports/{invalid_run_id}"
    response = page.goto(report_url)
    
    # Verify that the API returns 404 for missing resource
    expect(response).not_to_be_ok()
    assert response.status == 404
